articles.title: EVERYTHING WE COVERED AT SCHEMA BY FIGMA
articles.summary: Schema by Figma unveiled updates focused on design systems, including Extended collections for multi-brand management and Slots for customizable components launching in November. The Figma MCP server now brings Figma context to AI development tools, expanding to Starter plans with six free monthly calls. Additional features include npm package imports for Make prototypes, expanded variable modes (up to 20 for Organization plans), and JSON import/export for variables.

articles.title: ADOBE UNVEILS FIREFLY 5 WITH PHOTOREALISTIC IMAGES AND LAYERED PROMPT-BASED EDITING
articles.summary: Adobe launched Firefly 5, its latest AI image generation model, featuring native 4MP resolution, enhanced human rendering, and new prompt-based layered editing capabilities that let creators modify specific elements without regenerating entire images. The company is also expanding Firefly to include third-party models from partners like Topaz and ElevenLabs, while making Firefly Custom Models available to all creators through a private beta waitlist. These announcements were made at Adobe Max 2025, where the company emphasized commercially safe AI tools designed to enhance creative productivity across images, video, and audio.

articles.title: GRAMMARLY REBRANDS TO ‘SUPERHUMAN,' LAUNCHES A NEW AI ASSISTANT
articles.summary: Grammarly has rebranded as “Superhuman” following its July acquisition of the email client by the same name. It will keep the Grammarly product name intact for now. Alongside the rebrand, the company has launched an AI assistant called Superhuman Go, which integrates with tools like Gmail, Google Drive, and Jira to offer writing help and perform productivity tasks.

articles.title: WHY STEALING DESIGN WORKS
articles.summary: Perplexity's AI browser Comet succeeded by deliberately copying Chrome's familiar interface rather than innovating with radical new designs like Arc and Dia, which struggled with user adoption. The strategy follows Jakob's Law (users prefer products that work like ones they already know), allowing people to switch browsers without learning new interactions while quietly integrating AI features. Instead of pursuing original design for novelty's sake, Comet prioritized invisible ease and useful functionality, proving that copying familiar patterns and then innovating subtly often beats flashy redesigns that create friction.

articles.title: ADOBE PUTS AI IN EVERYTHING, EVERYWHERE, ALL AT ONCE
articles.summary: At Adobe Max 2025, Adobe announced it's opening its Creative Cloud apps—like Photoshop and Premiere Pro—to third-party AI models, including Google's, emphasizing “choice” over exclusivity to Firefly. The company also unveiled new AI video, music, and voice tools, chat-based assistants, and tighter YouTube Shorts integration, aiming to keep creators inside Adobe's ecosystem as AI reshapes creative work.

articles.title: AS AI BECOMES AGI, DESIGN HAS A CRITICAL ROLE TO PLAY
articles.summary: Human-level AI could arrive within the next decade, potentially displacing billions of workers and triggering global economic and social disruption. Designers and strategists should act now by influencing policy, promoting preparedness, and shaping a future that safeguards human dignity, purpose, and social stability.

articles.title: CREATE PRODUCTION-READY WEBSITES BY CHATTING WITH AI
articles.summary: 10Web's AI Builder helps users build or recreate any website with AI in minutes. Customize it with the Elementor-based drag-and-drop editor.

articles.title: TRANSFORM YOUR IDEAS INTO DYNAMIC VIDEOS
articles.summary: Transform your ideas into captivating videos with just a few clicks. Hailuo Video harnesses advanced AI technology to generate high-quality videos.

articles.title: A DIRECTORY OF DESIGNERS, FOR DESIGNERS
articles.summary: Explore how designers found their path into tech or share your own to inspire others.

articles.title: TACKLING COMMON UX HURDLES WITH AI
articles.summary: AI capabilities enable solutions to longstanding UX problems by leveraging models' strengths rather than just adding chat interfaces to existing applications. Instead of empty states and tutorials, AI can generate working content that immediately engages users and supports learning through editing. Natural language understanding enables conversational search to replace complex filtering systems, while AI's ability to process unstructured inputs, such as images or PDFs, eliminates traditional form-filling requirements by automatically extracting and structuring data.

articles.title: DESIGNING SOFTWARE FOR THINGS THAT ROT
articles.summary: Fermento is a fermentation-tracking app that applies HACCP (Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Points) food safety frameworks to home fermentation projects, treating the process as monitored infrastructure rather than simple cooking. The app uses phase-based state machines to evaluate measurements such as pH and humidity against context-specific constraints, automatically flagging deviations and generating compliance documentation for high-risk ferments like cured meats while remaining useful as a simple diary for forgiving projects like sauerkraut. Unlike typical software optimized for uptime and consistency, the system is designed around controlled drift and variance, tracking trends over time to help fermenters distinguish between harmless fluctuations and dangerous deviations.

articles.title: HOW DESIGN LEADERS WIN OVER ORGANIZATIONS THAT DON'T TRUST UX
articles.summary: When joining a company that's lost faith in UX, the challenge isn't fixing bad design—it's rebuilding broken trust. To restore credibility, designers must understand why stakeholders became disillusioned, align UX work with business goals, deliver consistent small wins, and communicate impact in terms of value—like revenue or efficiency—rather than design metrics.

articles.title: STUDIO HEADS SPEAK OUT ON HOW AI IS RESHAPING THE CREATIVE PROCESS
articles.summary: AI primarily accelerates early-stage creative work, such as research and concepting, but cannot replace human judgment in distinguishing good ideas from poor ones.

articles.title: POP CULTURE REDUX: VIVID AND HUMOROUS ILLUSTRATIONS OF NICLAS M.
articles.summary: Guatemalan artist Niclas M. (Monkeymouthart) creates kitsch and lowbrow illustrations featuring vibrant colors, surrealism, and playful pop culture references using both traditional and digital methods.

articles.title: FAST IS A MOAT
articles.summary: True speed in design isn't about rushing—it's about rapidly turning ideas into tangible results that drive alignment and progress.
